stage.subtitle_ar: أوّل خطواتكِ نحو التفكير كمبرمِجة: متغيّرات وقيم، وحلقات ودوال، وكيف نصطاد الأخطاء ونصلحها بهدوء.

content.intro_ar: المتغيّرات هي أوّل لبنة في كل برنامج، وستحتاجينها في كل سطر تكتبينه في تخصّصكِ التقني. في هذا الدرس ستتعرّفين على المتغيّر بوصفه «صندوقًا» له اسم يخزّن قيمة، وكيف تُسنِدين القيم وتغيّرينها. تعلّمي كلماتها بالإنجليزية، فهي مفاتيح ستقرئينها في كل كود تقريبًا.

vocab.meaning_en: a named place in code that stores a value
vocab.example: A variable can hold your name or your age.

vocab.meaning_en: the information that a variable holds
vocab.example: The value of the variable is 18.

vocab.meaning_en: to keep data so you can use it later
vocab.example: A variable can store a number.

vocab.meaning_en: the label you give to a variable
vocab.example: I give the variable a clear name.

vocab.meaning_en: a value like 5 or 42
vocab.example: Ten is a number.

vocab.meaning_en: letters or words saved as a value
vocab.example: Her name is saved as text.

vocab.meaning_en: a value made of letters or words
vocab.example: "Atyaf" is a string.

vocab.meaning_en: to give a value to a variable
vocab.example: We assign the number 5 to the variable.

vocab.meaning_en: to give a variable a new value
vocab.example: You can change the value later.

vocab.meaning_en: the instructions we write for a computer
vocab.example: She writes code every day.

vocab.meaning_en: the information a program works with
vocab.example: The program saves the data.

vocab.meaning_en: a simple picture of a variable
vocab.example: Think of a variable as a small box.

paragraphs: In code, a variable is like a small box. The box has a name, and it can store a value. The value can be a number, like 10, or text, like "Atyaf". When you write code, you give the box a name and you put data inside it.

To assign a value means to put it in the box. For example, you can write age = 18. Now the variable "age" has the value 18. Later, you can change this value. If you write age = 19, the box keeps the new number and forgets the old one.

Variables help the computer remember data. A program can store many values at the same time: your name, your score, or the time. Good names make code easy to read, so we use clear names like "score" and not just "x". A variable is a simple idea, but it is one of the first big ideas in programming.

key_phrases.en: Give the variable a clear name.
Assign a value to the variable.
The value can be a number or text.
key_phrases.ar: القيمة قد تكون رقمًا أو نصًّا.

key_phrases.en: You can change the value later.

comprehension.question: In the reading, what is a variable like?
options: A small box with a name
comprehension.correct: A small box with a name
comprehension.explanation_ar: شبّه الدرس المتغيّر بصندوق صغير له اسم يخزّن قيمة.

comprehension.question: What can a value be?
comprehension.explanation_ar: القيمة قد تكون رقمًا مثل 10 أو نصًّا مثل الاسم.

comprehension.question: What does "assign" mean?
options: To put a value in a variable
comprehension.correct: To put a value in a variable
comprehension.explanation_ar: الإسناد (assign) يعني وضع قيمة داخل المتغيّر.

comprehension.question: After age = 19, what value does age have?
comprehension.explanation_ar: عند تغيير القيمة يحتفظ الصندوق بالرقم الجديد 19 وينسى القديم.

comprehension.question: Why do we use clear names like "score"?
options: To make code easy to read
comprehension.correct: To make code easy to read
comprehension.explanation_ar: الأسماء الواضحة تجعل قراءة الكود أسهل.

task.prompt_en: Write 3–4 sentences about two variables you could use in a program. Give each one a name and a value.
task.prompt_ar: اكتبي ٣–٤ جُمل عن متغيّرين يمكنكِ استخدامهما في برنامج. أعطي كلًّا منهما اسمًا وقيمة.
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي ٣ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، مثل variable وvalue وstore.

content.intro_ar: بعد أن عرفتِ المتغيّرات، حان وقت جعل الحاسوب يعمل بدلًا عنكِ. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّمين الحلقات التي تكرّر العمل، والدوال التي تكتبينها مرّة وتعيدين استخدامها. هاتان الفكرتان يعتمد عليهما المبرمجون يوميًا، وستفتحان لكِ باب التفكير كمبرمِجة حقيقية.

vocab.meaning_en: a way to repeat code many times
vocab.example: A loop can print the numbers from 1 to 100.

vocab.meaning_en: to do the same action again
vocab.example: The loop repeats the same step.

vocab.meaning_en: a named block of code that does one job
vocab.example: This function adds two numbers.

vocab.meaning_en: to run a function by its name
vocab.example: You can call the function whenever you need it.

vocab.meaning_en: the data you give to a function
vocab.example: The function takes two numbers as input.

vocab.meaning_en: the result a function gives back
vocab.example: The output is the sum of the numbers.

vocab.meaning_en: a named input that a function expects
vocab.example: The function has one parameter for the name.

vocab.meaning_en: to send a result back from a function
vocab.example: The function returns the total.

vocab.meaning_en: to use the same code again
vocab.example: We reuse the function in many places.

vocab.meaning_en: a group of lines that belong together
vocab.example: The loop runs one block of code.

vocab.meaning_en: a test that is true or false
vocab.example: The loop stops when the condition is false.

vocab.meaning_en: one action in a set of instructions
vocab.example: The loop does one step at a time.

reading.title_en: Do It Again, Write It Once
paragraphs: Computers are very good at doing the same task many times without getting tired. When we want to repeat an action, we use a loop. A loop runs a block of code again and again until a condition becomes false. For example, if you want to print the numbers from 1 to 100, you do not write a hundred lines. Instead, you write one loop, and the computer repeats the step for you.

A function is a named block of code that does one clear job. You write it once, and then you can call it whenever you need it. This idea is called reuse, and it keeps programs short and clean. A function often takes an input through a parameter, does some work, and then returns an output. For instance, a function called "add" might take two numbers and return their sum.

Loops and functions are often used together. A function can contain a loop, and a loop can call a function on every step. Because the logic has been written in one place, the code is easier to read and to fix. Professional developers rely on these two ideas every day, so if you understand them well, you have taken a real step toward thinking like a programmer.

key_phrases.en: A loop repeats a block of code.
A function does one clear job.
Call the function when you need it.
The loop runs until the condition is false.
key_phrases.ar: تعمل الحلقة حتى يصبح الشرط غير صحيح.

key_phrases.en: Write it once, then reuse it.

comprehension.question: What does a loop do?
comprehension.explanation_ar: الحلقة تكرّر تنفيذ كتلة من الكود عدّة مرّات.

comprehension.question: In the reading, when does a loop stop?
comprehension.explanation_ar: تتوقّف الحلقة عندما يصبح الشرط غير صحيح (false).

comprehension.question: Why do we use functions?
options: To write code once and reuse it
comprehension.correct: To write code once and reuse it
comprehension.explanation_ar: الدالة تُكتب مرّة ثم يُعاد استخدامها، ممّا يبقي الكود قصيرًا ونظيفًا.

comprehension.question: What does a function give back?
How does a function usually receive its input?
How can loops and functions work together?
options: A function can contain a loop
They must be on different computers
Only one can be used per program
comprehension.correct: A function can contain a loop
comprehension.explanation_ar: يمكن أن تحتوي الدالة على حلقة، وأن تستدعي الحلقة دالة في كل خطوة.

task.prompt_en: Explain in 4–5 sentences what a loop and a function are, and give one simple example of each.
task.prompt_ar: اشرحي في ٤–٥ جُمل ما الحلقة وما الدالة، مع مثال بسيط لكلٍّ منهما.
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي على الأقل ٤ كلمات من كلمات الدرس، مثل loop وfunction وcall وreturn.

content.intro_ar: لا يوجد مبرمج لا يقع في الأخطاء؛ الفرق أنّ المحترف يعرف كيف يصطادها ويصلحها. في هذا الدرس ستفهمين ما الـ bug، وكيف تقرئين رسالة الخطأ، وتتبّعين السبب حتى الوصول إلى الحلّ. تعلّمي أنّ الخطأ ليس فشلًا، بل خطوة طبيعية في طريق كل مبرمِجة ناجحة.

vocab.meaning_en: a mistake in code that makes it work wrong
vocab.example: There is a bug in my program.

vocab.meaning_en: to find and fix bugs in code
vocab.example: I need to debug this function.

vocab.meaning_en: a problem that stops the code from working right
vocab.example: The screen shows an error.

vocab.meaning_en: to repair the code so it works
vocab.example: She found the bug and fixed it.

vocab.meaning_en: to check whether the code works
vocab.example: Always test your code in small parts.

vocab.meaning_en: to make a program work
vocab.example: Run the program to see the result.

vocab.meaning_en: when a program stops suddenly
vocab.example: The app crashed and closed.

vocab.meaning_en: text the computer shows about a problem
vocab.example: Read the error message carefully.

vocab.meaning_en: the reason for a problem
vocab.example: The cause was a wrong name.

vocab.meaning_en: the way to fix a problem
vocab.example: Once you know the cause, the solution is often small.

vocab.meaning_en: something that is not working
vocab.example: I have a small problem in my code.

vocab.meaning_en: one row of code
vocab.example: The error is on line 12.

paragraphs: Every programmer writes code that does not work at first. A mistake in the code is called a bug, and the process of finding and fixing it is called debugging. Bugs are completely normal; even expert developers create them every day. So when your program shows an error, do not feel bad, because it is simply part of the job.

When you run a program and it crashes, the computer often shows an error message. This message is a gift, not a punishment, because it usually tells you the line where the problem is. Read it slowly, and then try to find the cause: maybe a name is spelled wrong, or a value is missing. Once you understand the cause, the solution is often small.

A good way to debug is to test your code in small parts. Instead of writing a hundred lines and then running everything at once, run your program often. If you have added only a little new code and a bug appears, you already know where to look. Being patient and curious matters more than being fast. Debugging teaches you to think clearly, and it is one of the most useful skills you will build as a programmer.

key_phrases.en: A bug is a mistake in the code.
Find the cause before the fix.
Test your code in small parts.

comprehension.question: What is a bug?
comprehension.explanation_ar: الـ bug هو خطأ في الكود يجعله يعمل بشكل غير صحيح.

comprehension.question: What is debugging?
comprehension.explanation_ar: التنقيح (debugging) هو عملية إيجاد الأخطاء وإصلاحها.

comprehension.question: Why is an error message useful?
options: It makes the code run faster
It often shows the line with the problem
comprehension.correct: It often shows the line with the problem
comprehension.explanation_ar: رسالة الخطأ مفيدة لأنّها غالبًا تدلّكِ على السطر الذي فيه المشكلة.

comprehension.question: According to the reading, how should you feel when you see a bug?
options: It is normal, not a reason to feel bad
comprehension.correct: It is normal, not a reason to feel bad
comprehension.explanation_ar: الأخطاء طبيعية ويقع فيها حتى الخبراء، فلا داعي للشعور بالسوء.

comprehension.question: What is a good way to debug?
options: Test the code in small parts
comprehension.correct: Test the code in small parts
comprehension.explanation_ar: من الأفضل اختبار الكود على أجزاء صغيرة وتشغيله كثيرًا.

comprehension.question: What should you find before the solution?
comprehension.explanation_ar: افهمي سبب المشكلة أولًا، وعندها يصبح الحلّ غالبًا بسيطًا.

task.prompt_en: Write 4–5 sentences about a time something did not work (a phone, an app, or code) and how you tried to fix it.
task.prompt_ar: اكتبي ٤–٥ جُمل عن مرّة لم يعمل فيها شيء (هاتف أو تطبيق أو كود) وكيف حاولتِ إصلاحه.
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي على الأقل ٤ كلمات من كلمات الدرس، مثل bug وerror وfix وtest.
